== 2...Nf6 · Mediterranean defence ==

Black's main idea is to overextend White's pawns. 

[[/3. e5|'''3. e5''']] is the main line. White agrees to take space in the centre with tempo. Black's knight routes to ...Nd5 then ...Ng6. This resembles the [[Chess Opening Theory/1. e4/1...Nf6|Alekhine defence]], except Black has already committed to ...e6 instead of ...d6, which is more usual in the Alekhine. 

[[/3. Nc3|'''3. Nc3''']] seeks a return to something more standard. 3...d5 transposes into the classical French. 

'''3. Nf3?''' gives up the e pawn to develop with tempo. 3...Nxe4 4. Bd3 Nf6 and White has some compensation, though probably not enough.
